channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government eyes ASEAN fuel‑sharing pact as fuel prices ease but volatility lingers
item.description: Sa balita, ipinakita ng Government ng bansa ang mga bagong benchmark presyo ng langis at fuel sa Metro Manila para sa April 16, 2026, na nagpapatuloy mula sa huling rollback sa April 14. Ang mga avera...

item.title: FKF Urged to Clarify Rewards and Status of Women’s Football Competitions
item.description: NAIROBI — Violet Kerubo Momanyi, the FKF National Executive Committee Women Representative, has raised serious concerns over the unclear state of women’s football competitions in Kenya. In a letter to...

item.title: Rising LPG Costs Hit Small Restaurants, Force Menu Adjustments
item.description: Mahirap talaga para sa mga may-ari ng mga carinderia ngayon dahil sa taas ng presyo ng LPG at mga bilihin. Ayon kay Joan Hipolito, president ng Philippine Association of Stores and Carinderia Owners, ...

item.title: Aliko Dangote and Global Leaders Make TIME 100 Most Influential List for 2026
item.description: Aliko Dangote, Nigeria’s foremost industrialist, don once again make the 2026 TIME100 list of the world’s most influential people. The list, wey TIME Magazine release on April 15, feature leaders from...

item.title: Presenter Lewis Nicholls Opens Up About Past Sexual Assault and His Journey to Healing
item.description: British television presenter Lewis Nicholls has publicly shared that he was sexually assaulted twelve years ago by two men in their fifties. Now 32, Nicholls revealed the experience in an emotional In...

item.title: Actress Joy Harmon, known for Cool Hand Luke and later bakery career, dies aged 87 after pneumonia
item.description: Joy Harmon, the American actress celebrated for her memorable role as Lucille in the 1967 film *Cool Hand Luke*, has died aged 87 at her home near Los Angeles. Her family confirmed she passed away aft...

item.title: Trump's Threats Against Federal Reserve Leadership Raise Concerns Over Economic Stability and Inflation Management
item.description: President Donald Trump has repeatedly threatened to remove Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, raising serious concerns about the independence of the central bank and its ability to manage inflation ...

item.title: How Africa Can Manage Its Ocean Resources Fairly and Sustainably
item.description: The article highlights the growing competition for Africa’s marine resources and how such pressures are triggering conflicts that threaten both livelihoods and sustainable development. With the rapid ...

item.title: Tanzania’s honey industry grows with focus on exports and branded products
item.description: Tanzania’s beekeeping sector dey grow well as international demand for natural honey dey increase. Many local firms now dey shift from just selling raw honey to producing processed and branded product...

item.title: CRDB Bank expands lending across East and Central Africa, supporting key sectors
item.description: CRDB Bank Group has significantly increased its lending footprint in East and Central Africa, reaching Sh13.7 trillion by 2025. The bank has targeted sectors that are crucial for economic development ...

item.title: Man in small boat attempts to enter Israeli embassy in London over Gaza conflict
item.description: A man arriving in London via a small boat attempted to gain entry to the Israeli embassy as a response to casualties in Gaza. Authorities confirmed that the individual was motivated by anger over rece...

item.title: Major fire disrupts fuel production at Viva Energy refinery in Geelong
item.description: A major fire erupted late Wednesday night at Viva Energy’s refinery in Corio, Geelong, one of only two oil refineries operating in Australia. The blaze broke out just after 11pm, sparking multiple exp...

item.title: Residents Clash With Police Over Poor Conditions at Ishiara Hospital, Two Dead
item.description: On Tuesday, violent clashes erupted in Mbeere North after residents protested the deteriorating state of Ishiara Level Four Hospital. The unrest disrupted transport and blocked key roads. Police moved...

item.title: Sam Kerr Nets 75th Goal as Matildas Defeat Kenya 2-0 in Nairobi
item.description: Sam Kerr reached a milestone with her 75th international goal as the Matildas secured a 2-0 win over Kenya in the FIFA Series final at Nairobi's Nyayo Stadium. Despite the scoreline, the match was tou...

item.title: Trump Administration Approves Pipeline Expansions for Canadian Oil Transport
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has issued a series of permits aimed at facilitating the transportation of crude oil and petroleum products between the United States and Canada. These approvals include a ...

item.title: Grayson Perry Explores the Impacts of Artificial Intelligence in New Documentary
item.description: Grayson Perry presents a thought-provoking three-part documentary, 'Grayson Perry Has Seen the Future', examining artificial intelligence and its potential consequences. The programme explores various...

item.title: Democrats Question White House Budget Director Over Alleged Illegal Spending Cuts
item.description: White House budget director Russell Vought faced intense scrutiny from Democrats during a recent hearing, where he was accused of repeatedly violating the law through illegal withholding of congressio...

item.title: Developments in the Middle East Conflict as of April 16, 2026
item.description: The article published by GMA News on April 16, 2026, delivers continuing coverage of the escalating conflict in the Middle East. It compiles verified updates from international and regional news agenc...

item.title: Techie Duped for ₹1.46 Lakh in Fake IPL Ticket Scheme
item.description: A tech professional in Bihar, India, has fallen victim to a sophisticated ticket scam involving fake tickets for an Indian Premier League (IPL) match between Royal Challengers Bangalore (RCB) and Chen...

item.title: Police OCS in Ishiara recalled as investigations continue into Embu protest shootings
item.description: The Inspector General of Police, Douglas Kanja, has directed the recall of the Officer Commanding Station (OCS) in Ishiara, Embu County, following the fatal shootings of two people during protests ove...
